findings.title: Missing security headers: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy
findings.description: Several recommended HTTP security headers are absent on responses. Without HSTS the API can be downgrade-attacked; without X-Content-Type-Options browsers may MIME-sniff JSON; without X-Frame-Options/CSP, embedded contexts are not restricted.
findings.reproduction: curl -I https://backend-agent-test.onrender.com/ and inspect response headers.
findings.expected: Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy headers present.
findings.actual: Missing: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Referrer-Policy
findings.suggested_fix: Add a middleware that injects HSTS, nosniff, frame-deny, CSP and Referrer-Policy headers on every response.

findings.title: CORS allows wildcard origin together with credentials
findings.description: The server returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * together with Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true. Browsers will refuse this combination, but proxies / non-browser clients will not, and it indicates the CORS policy was not threat-modelled.
findings.reproduction: GET / and inspect Access-Control-Allow-Origin and Access-Control-Allow-Credentials.
findings.expected: ACAO must be a specific origin (or omit ACAC) when credentials are allowed.
findings.suggested_fix: Echo a vetted origin list rather than '*' when credentials are enabled, or set credentials=false.

findings.title: Server fingerprint disclosed via headers
findings.description: Response headers reveal the underlying application server implementation, which assists attackers in selecting CVEs.
findings.expected: No internal server identification in production responses.
findings.suggested_fix: Strip the x-render-origin-server / Server header at the edge.

findings.title: Framework default endpoint /openapi.json exposed but not documented
findings.description: /openapi.json is reachable in production. It is not part of the supplied OpenAPI spec but is a FastAPI default. Either disable it in production or list it in the spec.

info.title: Mini Social API
info.description: A small social-media API used as a backend testing exam target.

post.description: Register a new user. Returns an access token on success.
Authenticate with username and password. Returns an access token.

patch.description: Update the current user's profile (bio, age).

get.description: List posts in the public feed, paginated via `limit` and `offset`.

post.description: Create a new post authored by the current user.

get.description: Retrieve a single post by id.

patch.description: Update the body of a post owned by the current user.

delete.description: Delete a post owned by the current user.

schema.title: Response List Comments Posts  Post Id  Comments Get

post.description: Like a post on behalf of the current user.
Follow the user with the given id.

findings.expected: /openapi.json declared in openapi.json or disabled.
findings.suggested_fix: Set docs_url=None / redoc_url=None in production or document them.

findings.title: Framework default endpoint /docs exposed but not documented
findings.description: /docs is reachable in production. It is not part of the supplied OpenAPI spec but is a FastAPI default. Either disable it in production or list it in the spec.
findings.expected: /docs declared in openapi.json or disabled.
findings.suggested_fix: Set docs_url=None / redoc_url=None in production or document them.

findings.title: Framework default endpoint /redoc exposed but not documented
findings.description: /redoc is reachable in production. It is not part of the supplied OpenAPI spec but is a FastAPI default. Either disable it in production or list it in the spec.

response.body:
<!DOCTYPE html>
    <html>
    <head>
    <title>Mini Social API - ReDoc</title>
    <!-- needed for adaptive design -->
    <meta charset="utf-8"/>
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
    
    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css?family=Montserrat:300,400,700|Roboto:300,400,700" rel="stylesheet">
    
    <link rel="shortcut icon" href="https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/img/favicon.png">
    <!--
    ReDoc doesn't change outer page styles
    -->
    <style>
      body {
        margin: 0;
        padding: 0;
      }
    </style>
    </head>
    <body>
    <noscript>
        ReDoc requires Javascript to function. Please enable it to browse the documentation.
    </noscript>
    <redoc spec-url="/openapi.json"></redoc>
    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/redoc@next/bundles/redoc.standalone.js"> </script>
    </body>
    </html>

findings.expected: /redoc declared in openapi.json or disabled.
findings.suggested_fix: Set docs_url=None / redoc_url=None in production or document them.

findings.title: Tokens remain valid after /auth/logout
findings.description: After calling POST /auth/logout the same access token is still accepted by /users/me. Logout is therefore a no-op (no server-side revocation), which defeats user expectations for a session ending.
findings.reproduction: POST /auth/logout, then GET /users/me with same token
findings.expected: 401 Unauthorized after logout (token revoked).
findings.suggested_fix: Track a server-side token denylist or rotate a per-session secret on logout.

findings.title: IDOR: a non-owner can PATCH another user's post
findings.description: User Bob successfully edited a post authored by Alice. Posts should be editable only by their author.
findings.reproduction: Login as bob, PATCH /posts/<alice_post_id> with new body.
findings.expected: 403 Forbidden (non-owner) or 404
findings.suggested_fix: Compare post.author_id to current user before applying mutation.

findings.title: PATCH /users/me response leaks sensitive fields: ['password_hash']
findings.description: The PATCH /users/me response body contains sensitive fields ['password_hash']. UserPrivate explicitly does not declare a password / hash field — these must never be serialized to the client.
findings.reproduction: PATCH /users/me with any body, inspect response keys.
findings.expected: UserPrivate fields only (id, username, email, role, bio, age).
findings.suggested_fix: Use a response_model that excludes the password_hash column.

findings.title: Mass assignment: PATCH /users/me allows role escalation
findings.description: Submitting {role: 'admin'} in PATCH /users/me sets the user's role to admin. The PATCH endpoint should only accept whitelisted fields (bio, age).
findings.expected: role unchanged; PATCH only accepts {bio, age}.
findings.suggested_fix: Define a strict UserUpdate Pydantic model that only includes editable fields.

findings.title: Mass assignment: PATCH /users/me allows email change
findings.description: PATCH /users/me only documents bio/age as editable; the API allowed the email field to be overwritten as well.
findings.expected: email unchanged
findings.suggested_fix: Restrict editable fields to {bio, age} via a typed Pydantic model.

findings.title: A user can follow themselves
findings.description: POST /users/<own_id>/follow succeeds; this is a logic flaw — a user cannot meaningfully follow themselves.
findings.expected: 400 Bad Request — cannot follow self.
findings.suggested_fix: Reject follow when target_id == requester_id.

findings.title: Liking the same post twice increments like count
findings.description: After liking the same post twice as a single user, like_count=2 (>1).
findings.reproduction: POST /posts/<id>/like twice with the same user.
findings.expected: Idempotent: each user contributes at most 1 like.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the like operation a uniqueness-constrained insert keyed by (user_id, post_id).

findings.title: Can like a non-existent post
findings.description: POST /posts/999999999/like returned a success status although the post does not exist.
findings.expected: 404 Not Found

findings.title: POST /posts accepts a non-string `body`
findings.description: Sending `body=12345` (integer) is accepted and stored as the integer 12345. Posts are documented as string-bodied content; the API does not validate the type and the value is round-tripped as an integer in the response.
findings.expected: 422 Unprocessable Entity

findings.title: Negative `limit` query parameter triggers a 500
findings.description: GET /posts?limit=-1 returns a 500 Internal Server Error rather than a validation error. Out-of-range query parameters should be rejected at the validation layer with 422.
findings.expected: 422 Unprocessable Entity
findings.suggested_fix: Apply Query(..., ge=0) on the limit parameter, and similarly bound offset.

findings.title: Negative age accepted on PATCH /users/me
findings.description: Setting age to -42 is accepted and persisted; age should be >=0.
findings.expected: 422 Validation Error
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Field(ge=0) constraint on UserUpdate.age.

findings.title: Form-encoded body to /auth/register returns 500
findings.description: Sending a form-encoded body returns a 500 — the API should respond 415 Unsupported Media Type or 422.

findings.title: Malformed JSON triggers a 5xx
findings.description: Garbage JSON in the request body should be a 400/422, not a 500.

findings.title: No rate limiting observed on /auth/login
findings.description: 20 failed login attempts in a tight burst all received non-429 responses. There is no apparent rate limit on the login endpoint, which enables brute-force credential attacks.
findings.reproduction: Fire 20 concurrent POST /auth/login attempts with a wrong password.
findings.expected: At least some responses with 429 Too Many Requests after a sustained burst.
findings.suggested_fix: Add per-IP and per-username rate limiting on /auth/login.

findings.title: No caching/validation headers on /posts
findings.description: Public GET endpoints expose no Cache-Control, ETag or Last-Modified, preventing client and proxy caching.

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

body.body: This is Bob test post for agent testing.
body.author: bob

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

body.body: This is Bob test post for agent testing.
body.author: bob

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

body.body: This is Bob test post for agent testing.
body.author: bob

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

body.body: This is Bob test post for agent testing.
body.author: bob

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

body.body: This is Bob test post for agent testing.
body.author: bob

body.body: This is Alice test post for agent testing.
body.author: alice

findings.expected: Cache-Control or ETag/Last-Modified set on read-only collections.

findings.title: OPTIONS preflight reflects wildcard ACAO for arbitrary Origin
findings.description: A preflight from Origin 'https://evil.example' is answered with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, accepting any cross-origin caller.
findings.expected: ACAO restricted to a vetted origin list.

findings.title: HEAD not supported on /posts
findings.description: HEAD on /posts returns 405. RFC 9110 expects HEAD to be supported wherever GET is.
findings.expected: 200 with no body

findings.title: OpenAPI declares 'authorization' as an optional header parameter
findings.description: The spec lists 'authorization' as an optional header parameter rather than declaring an HTTP-Bearer security scheme. This misrepresents the auth requirements: the endpoint does require a token in practice.
findings.expected: securitySchemes.bearerAuth declared and required on protected paths.
findings.actual: Authorization listed as an optional Header parameter.
findings.suggested_fix: Define components.securitySchemes.bearerAuth and apply security: [{bearerAuth: []}] on protected operations.

findings.title: /auth/register has no documented requestBody
findings.description: /auth/register requires username/password/email but the spec declares no requestBody — clients cannot generate a typed payload from the spec.
findings.expected: requestBody with a schema describing username/password/email.

findings.title: /auth/login has no documented requestBody
findings.description: /auth/login requires username/password/email but the spec declares no requestBody — clients cannot generate a typed payload from the spec.
findings.expected: requestBody with a schema describing username/password/email.

findings.title: Post shape differs between list and detail endpoints
findings.description: GET /posts (list) and GET /posts/{id} (detail) return different sets of fields — list keys=['author', 'author_id', 'body', 'created_at', 'id', 'like_count'], detail keys=['authorName', 'author_id', 'body', 'created_at', 'id', 'like_count']. Clients are forced to branch on which endpoint they used.
findings.reproduction: Compare keys of GET /posts[0] vs GET /posts/<id>
findings.expected: Same canonical post shape from both endpoints.

findings.title: Author display field is named differently in list vs detail
findings.description: GET /posts uses ['author'] but GET /posts/{id} uses ['authorName']. The same logical field has two different names depending on the endpoint.
findings.reproduction: Compare /posts[0] and /posts/<id>; note author key naming.
findings.expected: Consistent field name (e.g. 'author_username') everywhere.
findings.suggested_fix: Pick one canonical name and add a deprecated alias if needed.

findings.title: like_count has different types between list and detail
findings.description: GET /posts returns like_count as str (value='38'), GET /posts/{id} returns it as int (value=38). Strongly-typed clients will fail on whichever payload they did not expect.
findings.expected: like_count is an integer in both responses.
findings.suggested_fix: Cast like_count to int in the list serializer.

findings.title: GET /posts returns like_count as a string
findings.description: Counters should be numeric. The /posts list serializer emits like_count as a string ('38'). Numeric-typed clients (TypeScript, Swift, Kotlin) will fail to deserialize, and JSON-Schema generators will mistype downstream models.
findings.expected: "number"
findings.suggested_fix: Cast like_count to int in the list serializer (likely a SQL COUNT() returning a varchar).
